behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: this fails at the handoff between inventory-availability filtering and ranking (high-score items can leak if filter/order of operations is wrong). Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess is required, no AI/ML output-quality dependence, and no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher level because behavior text is about internal catalog/filter/rank logic, not external infrastructure or model non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Wire catalog source + availability filter + ranker in-process. Seed products where some out-of-stock items have highest relevance scores. Assert final returned list contains zero out-of-stock items.

behaviors.description: User preference filter excludes categories explicitly blocked in user settings.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure appears at interface between user-settings retrieval and category-filter application. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; correctness does not depend on AI/ML output; no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic settings-based filtering.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process settings store + recommendation pipeline. Configure blocked categories and mixed catalog. Assert blocked categories are absent from output for that user.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: this can fail at feature handoff between browsing-signal extraction and ranking weights, not only inside one scoring function. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement; no dependence on non-deterministic model quality; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected Agentic because behavior text describes deterministic weighting, not real model output variability.
behaviors.test_description: Assemble browsing-history provider + feature builder + ranker. Create candidate products with/without browsing signal while holding other factors constant. Assert signaled products rank above unsignaled ones.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page with stable ordering; requesting page 2 twice yields identical results if data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure boundary is between ranking output and pagination/sort stability logic across repeated requests. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI/ML quality dependence; no multi-step agentic chain. Rejected System since behavior text constrains unchanged internal data and repeated in-process requests.
behaviors.test_description: Run ranker + paginator together against fixed dataset and frozen user state. Request page 2 twice with identical parameters and assert same item IDs and order each time.

behaviors.description: Algorithm includes at least one product from an unbrowsed category (serendipity requirement).
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: this is a contract between user-history interpretation and diversification/reranking stage; failures emerge at that module boundary. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no real AI/ML quality judgment required by text; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected Agentic because requirement is a deterministic structural constraint (at least one novel category).
behaviors.test_description: Compose history module + ranker/diversification pass. Provide user history limited to categories A/B and candidate pool containing A/B/C. Assert final top-N contains at least one item from category C.

behaviors.description: New user with no history gets popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure is at handoff between cold-start detection and fallback popularity recommender. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement; no non-deterministic model output dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher levels because behavior text specifies deterministic fallback logic.
behaviors.test_description: Wire user-profile reader + cold-start gate + popularity source. Use a user with empty history and non-empty catalog popularity stats. Assert non-empty results and that ranking follows popularity order.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records correct product ID, position, and timestamp for each interaction.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: this fails at interface between recommendation presentation context and tracking-event persistence (position/product mapping and event payload). Integration-brake: can use injected clock and in-process repository; no required real OS/network/subprocess; no AI/ML dependence; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external telemetry infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process tracker with fake clock and event store. Simulate click on rendered recommendation list position. Assert stored event has exact product_id, rank position, and timestamp.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: latency depends on end-to-end pipeline interactions (filtering, scoring, pagination) not one function. Integration->System proof: real wall-clock performance under 100k items requires real process/runtime/OS scheduling and memory behavior; mocks hide true latency. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is performance SLA, not model-output quality or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real recommendation service/process with a realistic 100k-product dataset on target-like hardware/runtime. Measure p95 (or specified percentile) end-to-end request latency and assert it is <200ms.

behaviors.description: After user purchases a recommended product, subsequent recommendations deprioritize that product.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure is at handoff from purchase-event/state update to subsequent ranking computation. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required if event/state update is in-process; no AI/ML quality dependence stated; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected System because behavior text only requires sequential state transition and rerank.
behaviors.test_description: Connect purchase-history updater with recommendation engine using local in-process persistence. Generate baseline ranking, record purchase of a recommended item, request again, and assert purchased item rank drops.

behaviors.description: Recommendations are deterministic for unchanged user state across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: determinism can break at module boundaries (feature retrieval ordering, tie-break sorting, pagination composition), not just inside one function. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no non-deterministic AI/ML output dependency in behavior text; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected Agentic because requirement explicitly expects determinism for same state.
behaviors.test_description: Run full in-process recommendation pipeline twice with identical user state, catalog snapshot, and config; assert identical ordered product ID list across runs.
